descriptions.abstract:
Consider a two-player strategic-form game.
Row player has 5 actions: {A, B, C, D, E}.
Column player has 5 actions: {A, B, C, D, E}.
The payoff matrix (row player, col player) is:

                A         B         C         D         E
       A  (-3,-4)     (2,0)    (-4,1)    (-3,2)   (-2,-5)
       B  (-2,-3)     (4,0)    (3,-5)     (5,5)     (5,2)
       C   (-5,3)    (-4,0)     (4,1)   (-5,-2)   (-4,-2)
       D  (-4,-4)    (5,-3)    (-2,3)   (-3,-1)    (-4,4)
       E   (0,-2)     (1,2)     (3,4)     (1,4)    (5,-3)

Each cell shows (payoff to Row player, payoff to Column player).

Consider a two-player strategic-form game.
Buyer has 5 actions: {A, B, C, D, E}.

       D   (4,-5)     (1,4)   (-2,-5)   (-1,-2)     (3,0)
       E   (-2,2)    (0,-4)    (0,-3)    (0,-5)    (0,-5)

Each cell shows (payoff to Buyer, payoff to Seller).

Agent 2 has 3 actions: {A, B, C}.
The payoff matrix (row player, col player) is:

                A         B         C
       A   (-1,5)    (5,-4)    (-2,2)
       B  (-5,-5)    (4,-3)    (4,-5)
       C  (-1,-5)    (-2,1)   (-5,-5)
       D  (-4,-4)   (-1,-4)    (-3,3)
       E   (-5,4)    (3,-2)   (-1,-1)

Agent 2 plays the following mixed strategy: P(A)=0.3772, P(B)=0.2705, P(C)=0.3523.
What is Agent 1's best response?
